methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Albacore is a seed-stage Ocean & Maritime company founded by John Huddleston and Dante Vaisbort. The company has raised $6.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of five private peers in adjacent maritime and subsea sectors (Standard Subsea, Clippership, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, and Odin Dynamics); no public comparables exist in this nascent segment.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised of $6M as a value proxy, a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $16M. This multiplier reflects observed progression patterns in early-stage deeptech ventures moving from initial capital deployment into substantive product validation and customer traction phases.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, with a median estimate of $16M. The relatively narrow band reflects constraint to a single triangulation method; meaningful expansion of this range would require additional data on revenue, customer concentration, or comparable transaction multiples from the identified private peer set. Key uncertainties include Albacore's current burn rate, near-term revenue trajectory, and how meaningfully its operating metrics and market position track against the five private peers referenced. Substantiation of these inputs would refine confidence in the median estimate.
